prompt: Library systems desk here. Our nightly batch renders two plain-text artifacts: the overdue export we hand to the warehouse importer as a .sql file, and the 32-column tear-off slips for the returns-desk printer. Both have been rock solid for years. Last sprint someone modernized the old '+'-concatenated template constants as part of the Java version bump, and now the batch output is mangled in two ways. First, the importer rejects the .sql file — it parses line by line and says roughly half the lines are empty; when I open the file the statement is double-spaced, a blank line after every line except the last. Second, the desk printer's perforation check fails on every slip: it requires each record to be exactly 32 characters, and the first couple of lines of each slip now come up short (the ruled line and the item rows are fine, weirdly — it's just the top of the slip). The rendering logic itself wasn't supposed to change at all in that PR. TestMain.java pins both outputs byte-for-byte; run `java TestMain.java`. Fix the rendering so the tests pass and do not touch TestMain.java.
